name: Circular de Borredà Trail
name_original: Sant Jaume de Frontanyà - circular gravel desde Borredà
description: Cycle the difficult 22.1-mile Circular de Borredà Trail, gaining 2748 feet through Alt Berguedà's forests, valleys, and historic villages.
region_intro: Hiking around Borredà offers access to varied terrain in the Berguedà region of Catalonia, Spain. The landscape features a mix of forested areas, river valleys, and elevated points, providing diverse hiking experiences. Trails often lead through natural settings, connecting villages and points of interest. The area is suitable for exploring the natural environment on foot. Best hiking trails around Borredà The most popular hiking route is Gorg del Salt Waterfall loop from Borredà, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a notable waterfall, offering a scenic highlight. Another top favourite among local hikers is Salga Aguda – Summit of the Mountain loop from Vilada, a difficult 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path. This trail involves significant elevation gain, leading to a mountain summit with expansive views. Local hikers also love the Salga Aguda – Summit of the Mountain loop from la Quar, a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 50 minutes. Hiking around Borredà is defined by waterfalls, mountain summits, and forested paths. The network of routes offers options for easy hikes, moderate circular walks, and more challenging trails suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Borredà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 200 reviews. More than 1,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Borredà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Borredà?
A: There are over 130 hiking trails in the Borredà area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. This includes 36 easy routes, 75 moderate routes, and 26 difficult trails.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Borredà?
A: Yes, Borredà offers several easy trails suitable for families. These routes often feature gentle terrain and interesting sights. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, many of the 36 easy trails are suitable for a relaxed outing.

Q: Can I find moderate circular walks in the Borredà region?
A: Absolutely. A popular moderate circular walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg del Salt Waterfall loop from Borredà&lt;/a&gt;. This 6.1-mile (9.9 km) trail takes approximately 2 hours 50 minutes and features a notable waterfall, making it a scenic option for those seeking a moderate challenge.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while hiking around Borredà?
A: The Borredà area is rich in natural beauty. Hikers can explore various features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4139866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg del Salt Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2524486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrent de la Cabana Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2524507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomer Pool&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1100657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Merlès&lt;/a&gt; also offers picturesque river scenery.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain hikes with great views?
A: For experienced hikers, the region offers challenging routes to mountain summits. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3981778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salga Aguda – Summit of the Mountain loop from Vilada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 3.5-mile (5.6 km) path with significant elevation gain, leading to expansive views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3981829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salga Aguda – Summit of the Mountain loop from la Quar&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.4-mile (10.4 km) trail through mountainous terrain.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Borredà?
A: The trails in Borredà are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from nearly 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, including waterfalls, mountain summits, and forested paths, as well as the well-maintained routes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Borredà?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Borredà region. It is always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Borredà?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in Borredà, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring cooler temperatures and occasional snow at higher altitudes, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any longer, more demanding routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer and more demanding experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31861891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jaume de Frontanyà – Sant Jaume de Frontanyà loop from el Pla Rodó&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.2-mile (18 km) route. This trail involves significant distance and elevation, offering a substantial challenge.

Q: Are there any hikes that pass through villages or offer amenities?
A: Many routes in the Borredà area connect villages, allowing hikers to experience local culture and find amenities. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31865898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilada village loop from Vilada&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.5-mile (10.4 km) trail that starts and ends in the village of Vilada, providing access to local services.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks to look out for?
A: Beyond natural features, hikers can also discover interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4326401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lady's Oak&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument. Additionally, the region features several mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/578909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Merolla&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/940700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de Falgars&lt;/a&gt;, which offer scenic viewpoints.
tips: On the corner of Borredà the track is in good condition, along the corner of Castell de l'Areny a bit worse

An interesting fact about Sant Jaume de Frontanyà is that it has a population of only 30 inhabitants. Until recently the smallest town in Catalonia. Currently this town does not have a bar or a restaurant but it does have this fountain.

Riera de Merlès is a set of pools of different sizes and depths created by the erosion of water on the stone. In this section it is worth keeping an eye on the landscape, as it is beautiful. In the middle of summer, a dip is absolutely essential.

faqs.question: Is the Circular de Borredà Trail suitable for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: While the Komoot route is categorized for touring bicycles, the terrain in the Alt Berguedà region, particularly on sections of the broader GR 241, can be challenging. Expect unpaved and difficult segments where you might need to push your bike. It's generally more suited for gravel bikes or mountain bikes due to the varied terrain and significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse landscape with numerous ups and downs, passing through dense forests, along the picturesque Riera de Merlès, and through charming towns. While some parts may be smooth, be prepared for unpaved sections that can be rough and require good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This specific Komoot route, which is approximately 35.5 km long with about 838 meters of elevation gain, is estimated to take around 3 hours and 5 minutes to complete on a touring bicycle. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the type of bike, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Circular de Borredà Trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Borredà, making it convenient for parking. You can usually find parking within the village of Borredà itself, which serves as a common starting point for many routes in the area. Specific parking areas are typically well-marked.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a rich mix of natural beauty and historical sites. Highlights include the serene Riera de Merlès, the historic Sant Sadurní de Rotgers Church, and the charming village of Sant Jaume de Frontanyà. The route also offers panoramic views of the Berguedà region and the Baells reservoir, with glimpses of iconic peaks like Pedraforca and Cadí Moixeró on clear days.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Circular de Borredà Trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for cycling this trail. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in the valleys, while winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, making some sections less accessible.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for cycling in the Alt Berguedà region?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for cycling on public trails like the Circular de Borredà Trail in the Alt Berguedà region. However, always respect private property and any local regulations, especially if you venture off marked paths. There are no national park entrance fees for this area.

faqs.question: Is the Circular de Borredà Trail dog-friendly for cyclists?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the Berguedà region are dog-friendly for hikers, cycling with dogs, especially on a touring bicycle, can be more challenging due to the varied terrain and potential for unpaved sections. If you do bring your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you have a plan for their hydration and safety throughout the ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. Notably, it overlaps with parts of the Circular de Borredà trail (the broader GR 241), the Camí Ramader de Marina, and the Puigcerdà-Montserrat route. You might also encounter segments of the Sender de Puigcercós and the Camí ral de Ripoll a Berga.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the villages along and near the route, such as Borredà, Sant Jaume de Frontanyà, La Nou de Berguedà, and Vilada, offer options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially in smaller villages, and consider booking accommodation in advance during peak seasons.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring cycling trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring a well-maintained touring or gravel bike, appropriate cycling gear, a helmet, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, and a first-aid kit. A GPS device or a downloaded Komoot map is highly advisable for navigation, as some sections may not be clearly marked for cyclists.
